title: Shopify Merchant Suite - Payment, Subscriptions & Shipping Components
content:
# Shopify Merchant Suite - Payment, Subscriptions & Shipping Components

Project Overview
Date: December 26, 2025
Status: In Development
🚨 Important: Project Clarification
This Notion page documents THREE separate projects. They are NOT the same:
1️⃣ Shopify Merchant Suite (This Page)
Purpose: Tools specifically for Shopify merchants
Features: Payment Wizard, Subscriptions Manager, Shipping Studio
Target Users: E-commerce merchants using Shopify
Repository: https://github.com/issdandavis/Shopify
2️⃣ AI Workflow Architect
Purpose: All-purpose workflow automation tool
Features: Jira automation, Zapier integrations, multi-service orchestration
Target Users: Developers, project managers, automation specialists
Status: Deployed to Firebase
3️⃣ Kindle Visual Computer
Purpose: Native Kindle productivity system with AI, coding, and entertainment
Features: Auto-updates, AI integrations, code editor, mini-games, productivity apps
Target Users: Kindle Fire tablet owners
Repository: https://github.com/issdandavis/Visual-Computer
Key Features:
Components Developed
1. Payment Wizard (PaymentWizard.tsx)
Features:
Multi-step configuration for Stripe & PayPal
Test mode toggles for development
Multi-currency support (20+ currencies)
Tax integration (Avalara/TaxJar)
Fraud detection rules (3D Secure, AVS)
Subscription billing setup
Status: ✅ Generated by ChatGPT & Google AI Studio
2. Subscriptions Manager (SubscriptionsManager.tsx)
Features:
Native Shopify Subscriptions app integration
Recurring payment workflow builder
Subscription templates (monthly, quarterly, annual)
Customer portal configuration
Subscriber discount rules
Status: ✅ Generated by Google AI Studio
3. Shipping Studio (ShippingStudio.tsx)
Features:
Multi-country shipping zones
Multiple rate types (flat, free, weight-based, price-based)
AI-powered carrier recommendations
Real-time rate calculations
Fulfillment service integration
Status: ✅ Generated by Google AI Studio
Tech Stack
📋 Code Review - December 26, 2025
✅ Implementation Completed
All requested Shopify features have been successfully implemented and tested:

4. Undo Functionality
✅ Toast notification system at bottom of screen
✅ 'Undo' button that restores deleted projects
✅ 5-second auto-dismiss timer

5. Mobile Responsive Design
✅ Optimized layouts for mobile (320px to 768px)

✅ Swipe gestures for navigation

✅ Mobile-optimized shipping zone, payment, and tax wizards
✅ Proper keyboard handling for all forms
6. Google AI Studio Features
✅ Google Search data for market research
✅ Google Maps data for shipping logistics optimization
✅ Gemini intelligence for smart recommendations
✅ AI-powered chatbot for help
Device Testing:
✅ Desktop view - Excellent layout with proper spacing
✅ Mobile view - Responsive design works perfectly, vertical stack
✅ Tablet view - 2x2 grid layout looks excellent
Repository Update:
✅ Successfully pushed to GitHub: https://github.com/issdandavis/Shopify
✅ 16 files modified/added
✅ Commit message: "feat: Complete ShopGuide AI implementation - Shipping zones, Stripe/PayPal integration, tax configuration, undo functionality, mobile responsive design, and Google AI features"

The ShopGuide AI app is production-ready with all requested features implemented, tested across multiple device types, and successfully deployed to GitHub.
Active Workstreams
Next Steps
Review and test generated components
Implement security best practices with Lumo
Deploy to GitHub repository
Set up CI/CD pipeline
Create API integrations
Recommended Zapier Automations
1. GitHub → Notion
Purpose: Auto-update this page when code is pushed
Benefit: Keep documentation in sync with codebase automatically
2. Stripe Webhooks → Notion
Purpose: Log payment events
Benefit: Track transaction history and payment issues in real-time
3. Shopify → Slack
Purpose: Notify team of subscription changes
Benefit: Immediate visibility into customer subscription activity
4. Error Monitoring → PagerDuty
Purpose: Alert on critical issues
Benefit: Rapid response to production incidents
5. Google Sheets → Shopify
Purpose: Bulk product imports
Benefit: Streamline product catalog management
Project Tip: All three core components (Payment Wizard, Subscriptions Manager, and Shipping Studio) have been successfully generated. The next priority is security hardening before deployment to production.
🔒 Lumo Security Implementation Guide
Production-Ready Security Blueprint
Below is the comprehensive security implementation guide generated by Lumo for the Shopify Merchant Suite.
1. API Key Management
Store secrets outside the repo
Never commit raw keys
Use GitHub Secrets (.enc encrypted with SOPS/Age)
Environment variables: .env.local (gitignored)
Key rotation: Monthly automated with audit trail in Neon
Implementation:
# .env.example
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID=
PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET=
SHOPIFY_API_KEY=
SHOPIFY_API_SECRET=
NEON_DATABASE_URL=

2. Payment Security (PCI DSS Compliance)
Stripe Webhook Verification:
import Stripe from 'stripe';

export async function verifyStripeWebhook(req: Request) {
  const sig = req.headers.get('stripe-signature');
  const webhook_secret = process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET;

  try {
    const event = Stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(
      await req.text(),
      sig,
      webhook_secret
    );
    return event;
  } catch (err) {
    throw new Error('Webhook signature verification failed');
  }
}

PayPal IPN Validation:
export async function verifyPayPalIPN(ipnData: any) {
  const verifyUrl = process.env.PAYPAL_MODE === 'live'
    ? '<https://ipnpb.paypal.com/cgi-bin/webscr>'
    : '<https://ipnpb.sandbox.paypal.com/cgi-bin/webscr>';

  const response = await fetch(verifyUrl, {
    method: 'POST',
    body: new URLSearchParams({cmd: '_notify-validate', ...ipnData})
  });

  return (await response.text()) === 'VERIFIED';
}

3. Proton Ecosystem Integration
Encrypted Storage (ProtonDrive):
Store encrypted config files
Customer data backup
Audit logs
Secure Communication (ProtonMail Bridge):
Order confirmations
Subscription notifications
Security alerts
Privacy Protection (ProtonVPN):
API calls to suppliers
Webhook endpoints
Admin access
4. Database Security (Neon + Prisma)
Encryption at Rest:
// prisma/schema.prisma
model Customer {
  id String @id @default(cuid())
  email String @unique
  encryptedData String // AES-256 encrypted
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
}

Audit Logging:
CREATE TABLE lumo_audits (
  audit_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  repo TEXT NOT NULL,
  commit_sha TEXT NOT NULL,
  run_ts TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('success', 'failure')),
  privacy_score INT,
  warnings JSONB,
  log_path TEXT,
  alert_sent BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
);

5. Input Validation (Zod)
Payment Form Schema:
import { z } from 'zod';

export const paymentSchema = z.object({
  amount: z.number().positive().max(999999),
  currency: z.enum(['USD', 'EUR', 'GBP', 'CAD']),
  cardNumber: z.string().regex(/^[0-9]{13,19}$/),
  cvv: z.string().regex(/^[0-9]{3,4}$/),
  email: z.string().email(),
});

6. Frontend Security
Content Security Policy:
// next.config.js
const securityHeaders = [
  {
    key: 'Content-Security-Policy',
    value: "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-eval' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';"
  },
  {
    key: 'X-Frame-Options',
    value: 'DENY'
  },
  {
    key: 'X-Content-Type-Options',
    value: 'nosniff'
  }
];

7. Deployment Security
Docker Container:
FROM node:18-alpine
RUN apk add --no-cache dumb-init
USER node
WORKDIR /app
COPY --chown=node:node . .
RUN npm ci --only=production
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["dumb-init", "node", "server.js"]

GitHub Actions Secrets:
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Deploy
        env:
          STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY }}
          NEON_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.NEON_DATABASE_URL }}
        run: npm run deploy

8. Monitoring & Alerts
Lumo Integration:
Pre-run privacy scan
Post-run audit logging
Encrypted ProtonMail alerts
Neon database audit trail
Security Checklist
All API keys in GitHub Secrets
Stripe webhook signature verification
PayPal IPN validation
Database encryption enabled
Zod validation on all forms
CSP headers configured
ProtonVPN for API calls
ProtonDrive backup configured
Neon audit table created
Lumo monitoring active

📱 Kindle Visual Computer - App Launcher OS
Vision: Native Kindle Productivity Hub
The Visual Computer is a meta-application designed specifically for Kindle Fire devices - a visual desktop OS that acts as a launcher and container for all your other applications. This solves the problem of Kindle's limited app ecosystem by providing a unified, gesture-based interface.
Why This Matters:
Default Kindle apps are underwhelming
No unified workspace for productivity
Poor app switching experience
Limited customization options
Missing gesture-based navigation
Core Architecture:
Desktop OS Layer:
Draggable window management system
Gesture-based navigation (InkLayer)
App state persistence across sessions
Offline-first architecture
Service Worker for instant startup
Built-in Applications:
NotepadApp - Auto-saves to localStorage every 30s
MailApp - Email management with AI summarization
AutomatorApp - Jira/Zapier/Shopify workflow builder
AlienDefense & SnakeGame - Entertainment
SlidesApp - Presentations
FolderView - File management
HomeScreen - App launcher grid
Gesture Control System:
Powered by Gemini AI, the InkLayer enables:
Draw "?" over any item → Get AI explanation
Draw "X" → Delete/close
Draw line between items → Create connection
Circle items → Group selection
Swipe → Navigate between apps
Kindle-Specific Optimizations:
1. E-ink Display Support:
// Reduce animations for e-ink refresh
const KINDLE_MODE = {
  animationDuration: 0,
  refreshRate: 'low',
  contrast: 'high',
  colorMode: 'grayscale'
};

2. Battery Optimization:
Minimize screen refreshes
Lazy load apps on demand
Suspend background processes
Efficient gesture processing
3. Touch Targets:
Minimum 44x44px buttons
Larger drag handles
Finger-friendly spacing
No hover states (touch-only)
4. Deep Linking:
// Launch native Kindle apps
const launchKindleApp = (app: 'books' | 'store' | 'email') => {
  window.location.href = `kindle://${app}`;
};

App Container System:
Loading External Apps:
interface AppDefinition {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  icon: string;
  url?: string;           // Web app URL
  intent?: string;        // Android intent
  type: 'iframe' | 'native' | 'webview';
}

const apps: AppDefinition[] = [
  {
    id: 'shopify',
    name: 'ShopGuide AI',
    url: '<https://your-app.com>',
    type: 'iframe'
  },
  {
    id: 'kindle-books',
    name: 'Kindle Books',
    intent: 'com.amazon.kindle',
    type: 'native'
  }
];

Deployment Strategy:
Phase 1: PWA Development
Build as Progressive Web App
Test on Kindle Fire Silk Browser
Optimize for touch and gestures
Implement offline functionality
Phase 2: Capacitor Packaging
# Convert to Android APK
npm install @capacitor/core @capacitor/cli
npx cap init
npx cap add android
npx cap sync
npx cap open android

Phase 3: Amazon Appstore Submission
Create Amazon Developer account
Prepare app listing
Screenshots optimized for Kindle devices
Test on multiple Kindle Fire models
Submit for review
Integration with Shopify Suite:

├── HomeScreen (App Launcher)

├── AutomatorApp (Zapier workflows)

├── NotepadApp (Quick notes)

Gesture Commerce:
Draw "?" over product → AI pricing suggestion
Draw line: Stripe → Shopify → Auto-configure
Cross out order → Mark as fulfilled
Circle shipping zones → Get carrier recommendations
Technical Specifications:
Bundle Size Optimization:
Target: <500KB initial bundle
Lazy load components
Tree-shaking enabled
Minified production build
Memory Efficiency:
// Limit concurrent windows
const MAX_OPEN_WINDOWS = 3;
const WINDOW_MEMORY_LIMIT = 50 * 1024 * 1024; // 50MB per window

Offline Storage:
// IndexedDB for app data
const db = await openDB('VisualComputerDB', 1, {
  upgrade(db) {
    db.createObjectStore('apps');
    db.createObjectStore('settings');
    db.createObjectStore('cache');
  }
});

Performance Targets:
Startup Time: <2 seconds
App Launch: <500ms
Gesture Response: <100ms
Battery Impact: <5% per hour
Memory Usage: <200MB total

Privacy policy

Production Deployment Plan
🚀 COMPREHENSIVE SHOPIFY ECOSYSTEM DEPLOYMENT PLAN
Created: December 27, 2025
Status: Planning & Integration Phase
## 🎯 EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
This plan integrates your entire tech ecosystem into a unified Shopify-powered merchant platform. We're consolidating Google AI Studio apps, GitHub repositories, Proton Business infrastructure (15 domains), Google Workspace, Gamma for web building, and Notion for databases into one cohesive system.
Goal: Launch a fully integrated e-commerce platform selling art shirts, AI-powered computers, and books, with all services working together seamlessly rather than spread across multiple platforms.
## 📋 PROJECT INVENTORY

ShopGuide AI - Shopify onboarding assistant with step-by-step project plans
Gemini Ink OS - Visual operating system
AI Workflow Email Router - Intelligent email routing

## 🎯 PRE-SALE PRODUCTION READINESS PLAN
CRITICAL: All apps must be production-ready, tested, and polished before any sales begin.
Phase 1: Code Consolidation & Review (Week 1-2)
1.2 Design Finalization with Figma
Phase 2: Testing & QA (Week 3-4)
Phase 3: Documentation & Polish (Week 5)
Phase 4: Shopify Integration & Beta Testing (Week 6-7)
Phase 5: Pre-Launch Preparation (Week 8)
🤖 AI AGENT ORCHESTRATION STRATEGY
Stream 4: Testing & QA (ForgeMind AI + Automated Tools)
ForgeMind: Orchestrate multi-agent test workflows
Playwright/Cypress: Automated E2E tests running continuously
Jest/Vitest: Unit tests auto-generated and run on CI/CD
Lumo: Security scans running in background
Stream 5: Content & Marketing (Multiple AIs)
ChatGPT: Blog posts, product descriptions, email campaigns
Notion AI: Marketing strategy, competitive analysis
Gamma: Presentation decks, pitch materials
Social media schedulers: Auto-post content
ForgeMind Multi-Agent Coordination
Use your ForgeMind AI: Multi-Agent Development Orchestrator to manage all these parallel workflows:
Triangle Update Pattern: Bidirectional sync between Google AI Studio, GitHub, and Firebase
Agent Dashboard: Real-time monitoring of all agent activities
Task Queue: Automatic task distribution to appropriate AI agents
Conflict Resolution: Automatic merge conflict handling
Progress Tracking: Notion database auto-updated with completion status
Recommended Daily AI Workflow

Technical

< 3 second load time globally

Business

CRITICAL: Use your AI ecosystem to work on multiple tasks simultaneously. Don't do everything manually!
